title: Questions

headings.text: Questions
1 - Which MITRE technique ID describes Initial Access via a vulnerable mail server?
2 - Which Initial Access method relies on a user opening a malicious email attachment?
Phishing
3 - Which user seems to be most actively targeted by botnets?
Administrator
4 - Which IP managed to breach the host and enter the RDP session?
5 - Can you get the real Workstation Name (hostname) of the threat actor?
DESKTOP-QNBC4UU
6 - Run the www.zoom.com file from the Phishing Case 1 folder, which flag do you get?
THM{misleadingextension}
7 - From which URL does the malicious LNK download the next stage malware?
http://wp16.hqywlqpa.thm:8000/cgi-bin/f
8 - What is the name of the double-extension file you see there?
best-cat.jpg.exe
9 - Which file did the user download via the web browser?
C:\Users\Administrator\Downloads\top-cats.zip
10 - In which folder did the user unarchive the suspicious file?
C:\Users\Administrator\Pictures
11 - What is the process ID of the launched phishing malware?
12 - Which malicious domain did the malware try to connect to?
rjj.store
13 - Which USB file was launched by the user?
E:\Open Sandisk 4GB USB.exe
14 - Which suspicious file did the malware drop to the disk?
C:\Users\Public\Documents\winupdate.exe
15 - To which other USB did the malware propagate?
F:

workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
